headline: Postal Service plans 8% fuel surcharge as Iran war raises transport costs
summary: U.S. Postal Service is ready to charge parcel customers a fuel surcharge for the first time in its history because fuel prices are rising faster than it can change its base transportation rate. The post Postal Service plans 8% fuel surcharge as Iran war raises transport costs appeared first on FreightWaves.

headline: US Postal Service to Hike Prices as War Drives Up Fuel Costs
summary: (Bloomberg) -- The US Postal Service plans to raise prices to offset rising transportation expenses including the cost of fuel, a sign that the beleaguered mail carrier is feeling the strain of higher oil prices as the war in Iran continues.The USPS will increase prices by 8% on some packages until mid-January of next year, the USPS said in a statement Wednesday. The change will apply to its Priority Mail, Priority Mail Express, USPS Ground Advantage and Parcel Select offerings. Other products s

headline: Should UPS’s (UPS) Driver Buyout Retreat Prompt a Rethink of Its Efficiency and Margin Strategy?
summary: United Parcel Service (UPS) recently withdrew its latest driver buyout scheme in the Central Region after nearly 37 Teamsters local unions filed grievances alleging contract violations, prompting the union to press for the dismantling of the Driver Choice Program nationwide. This clash over the buyout and Driver Choice Program underscores how labor relations and contract enforcement could directly influence UPS’s operational flexibility and cost plans. headline: Postal Service to Impose First-Ever Fuel Surcharge on Packages
summary: The U.S. Postal Service will impose its first-ever surcharge on packages to cover the rising cost of fuel, according to two people familiar with the matter.  A spokesman for the Postal Service didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment.  Other parcel carriers, including  FedEx  and  United Parcel Service  have imposed fuel surcharges, as well as a basket of other surcharges and fees, for years.

raw.headline: Postal Service to Impose First-Ever Fuel Surcharge on Packages
raw.summary: The U.S. Postal Service will impose its first-ever surcharge on packages to cover the rising cost of fuel, according to two people familiar with the matter.  A spokesman for the Postal Service didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment.  Other parcel carriers, including  FedEx  and  United Parcel Service  have imposed fuel surcharges, as well as a basket of other surcharges and fees, for years.

headline: UPS retracts driver buyout option in 13 states under union pressure
summary: UPS will withdraw its buyout offer for drivers in 13 states because the Teamsters contract in that region gives local unions more rights to object to management’s move. The post UPS retracts driver buyout option in 13 states under union pressure appeared first on FreightWaves.

headline: Only 22% of global workers believe their jobs are safe, ADP finds
summary: Investing.com -- Only 22% of global workers strongly believe their jobs are safe from elimination, according to a new report from ADP Research released Wednesday, revealing widespread anxiety despite historically low unemployment rates worldwide.

headline: UPS opens $100 million Taiwan logistics hub to meet tech boom demand
summary: United Parcel Service opened a new $100 million logistics centre in ‌Taiwan on Wednesday, its largest in the Asia ‌Pacific, riding a wave of demand from tech companies.  Taiwan is home ​to TSMC, the world's top contract chip-maker and dominant supplier of the advanced semiconductors powering the boom in AI technologies.  UPS said the site in northern Taiwan's ‌Taoyuan, a short ⁠drive from the island's largest international airport, will also be used by Applied Materials, ⁠the largest U.S. semiconductor equipment maker, as an Asian distribution centre.
